questions.stem: This radiograph of a young child's lower leg shows open growth plates and a faint lucent line coursing obliquely through the tibial shaft without cortical displacement. What is the most likely diagnosis?

choices.label: D
choices.text: Toddler's fracture (nondisplaced spiral fracture of the tibia)

explanation.summary: A subtle nondisplaced oblique/spiral lucency of the tibial diaphysis in a young ambulating child is the classic 'toddler's fracture,' often barely visible acutely; there is no periosteal destruction (osteomyelitis/tumor) or tibial tubercle change (Osgood-Schlatter).
explanation.whyCorrect: A subtle nondisplaced oblique/spiral lucency of the tibial diaphysis in a young ambulating child is the classic 'toddler's fracture,' often barely visible acutely; there is no periosteal destruction (osteomyelitis/tumor) or tibial tubercle change (Osgood-Schlatter).
